name: Purchase Order Extractor powered by easybits

parameters.formDescription: Upload one or more purchase order PDFs to extract line items.

parameters.content:
## 📄 Form Intake
Collects the purchase order PDFs from the user. **Multiple files** can be uploaded in one submission. Only **.pdf** is accepted and at least one file is required.

## ✂️ Split & Rename
Splits a multi-file upload into **one item per PDF** and renames each binary to `data` (the name the Extractor expects). Also **resets the flag buffer** so warnings from a previous run don't carry over.

## 🔁 One PO at a time
Batch size **1**. The Extractor bundles all inputs into a single call, so each PDF must pass through on its own. The **loop** output processes each PO, the **done** output fires once at the end.

## 🚩 Build Warnings
Reads the flags collected during the run and builds the message for the end screen. If nothing was flagged, it reports a **clean extraction**. Runs once, on the loop's **done** output.

## 🤖 Extract with easybits
Reads the header fields and the full article table from one PDF. Output is wrapped in a `data` envelope. Field setup lives in the Extractor credential – see the main note for the field list and descriptions.

## 🧩 Flatten to Rows
Turns each PO into **one row per article**, stapling the header fields (PO Number, dates, Mark For, PR Number) onto every line. Adds the **Document Name**. Cleans every empty value (`null`, `NULL`, `""`, blanks) to an empty cell and **records a flag** per document if anything is missing.

## 📊 Write to Sheet
Appends every article row to the Google Sheet. **RAW** value mode keeps quantities and special characters literal. Columns map automatically – the header row in the sheet must match the field names exactly.

## ✅ End Screen
Shows the user the result after processing. Lists **which documents and which fields** may not have extracted fully, so they know what to double-check against the original PDF.

## 📦 Purchase Order Extractor (powered by easybits)

Upload one or more purchase order PDFs through a simple form. The workflow reads the **header details** and the **full article table** from each PO and writes everything into a Google Sheet – **one row per article**, with the parent PO fields repeated on each line so every row stands on its own. After processing, the end screen flags any document where a field came back empty, so nothing broken slips through unnoticed.

## 🔧 How the flow works
**Form** collects the PDFs → **Split** separates them into one item each → **Loop** feeds them in one at a time → **Extract** pulls the data → **Build Rows** flattens and flags → **Sheet** stores the rows. When the loop finishes, a **summary** is shown on the completion screen.

## 📝 Google Sheet setup
Create a sheet with these headers in row 1, matching exactly:
`Document Name | PO Number | PO Date | Delivery Date | Mark For | PR Number | Reference No. | Article | Unit | Quantity`
Connect it in the **Append** node and set Value Input mode to **RAW**.

## ☁️ Extractor setup (n8n Cloud)
The `@easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor` node is **verified** and available natively on n8n Cloud – just search for it when adding a node. Create an easybits account at https://extractor.easybits.tech/, generate an **API key**, and add it as the node credential. The free plan covers **10 fields per call** and **50 requests per month**, which is enough for this workflow.

## 🖥️ Extractor setup (self-hosted)
Install the community node from **Settings → Community Nodes → Install**, using the package name `@easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor`. Add your API key as the credential the same way. Full guide: extractor.easybits.tech/documentation/integration

## 📌 Extractor field configuration
Set an overall **document description** (a UAE hospitality purchase order with a header box and a numbered article table), then define these fields. Each description tells the Extractor where to look and to return `"null"` when a value is missing:
- **mark_for** – the "Mark For" value in the middle header column
- **po_number** – "PO Number" in the top-right box (format PO26-XXXXXX)
- **po_date** – "PO Date", DD.MM.YYYY, not the Delivery Date below it
- **delivery_date** – "Delivery Date", DD.MM.YYYY, not the PO Date above it
- **pr_number** – "PR Number" from the header box (lowercase "i", header only)
- **reference_no** – "Refrence No." in the header box, often blank
- **articles** – an **array** with sub-fields **article**, **unit**, **quantity**, one object per numbered table row. Note in the description that unnumbered lines like "2 Box" are notes, not separate articles.

## ⚠️ Good to know
The article names only stay clean because the descriptions tell the Extractor that **only numbered rows are real articles**. Reference No. is **not flagged** when empty, since it's blank on most POs and would create constant false warnings.
